en.extract:
The ochre-breasted catbird (Ailuroedus stonii) is a species of bird in the family Ptilonorhynchidae. It is found in southern New Guinea. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.
Until 2016, the ochre-breasted catbird was considered conspecific with the white-eared catbird. Martin Irestedt and colleagues examined the white-eared catbird species complex genetically and found there were three distinct lineages: the white-eared catbird (Ailuroedus buccoides) proper of the Bird's Head (Vogelkop) Peninsula, the ochre-breasted catbird (Ailuroedus stonii) of  the southern lowlands of New Guinea, and tan-capped catbird (Ailuroedus geislerorum) of the northern lowlands of New Guinea.


Subspecies
Two subspecies are recognised:

Ailuroedus stonii stonii – Sharpe, 1876: found in southeast New Guinea
Ailuroedus stonii cinnamomeus – Mees, 1964: found in southeast and southwest New Guinea


References

The '''ochre-breasted catbird''' ('''''Ailuroedus stonii''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Ptilonorhynchidae]]. It is found in southern [[New Guinea]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical dry [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.

Until 2016, the ochre-breasted catbird was considered conspecific with the [[white-eared catbird]]. Martin Irestedt and colleagues examined the white-eared catbird species complex genetically and found there were three distinct lineages: the white-eared catbird (''Ailuroedus buccoides'') proper of the [[Bird's Head Peninsula|Bird's Head (Vogelkop) Peninsula]], the ochre-breasted catbird (''Ailuroedus stonii'') of  the southern lowlands of [[New Guinea]], and [[tan-capped catbird]] (''Ailuroedus geislerorum'') of the northern lowlands of New Guinea.<ref name=irestedt16>{{cite journal | title=Contrasting phylogeographic signatures in two Australo-Papuan bowerbird species complexes (Aves: ''Ailuroedus'')|author1=Irestedt, Martin |author2=Batalha-Filho, Henrique |author3=Roselaar, Cees S. |author4=Christidis, Les |author5=Ericson, Per G. P. | journal = Zoologica Scripta |date=2016 |volume=45 |issue=4 |pages=365–379 |doi=10.1111/zsc.12163}}</ref>

==Subspecies==
Two [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=irestedt16/>
* ''Ailuroedus stonii stonii'' – <small>[[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1876</small>: found in southeast New Guinea
* ''Ailuroedus stonii cinnamomeus'' – <small>[[Gerlof Mees|Mees]], 1964</small>: found in southeast and southwest New Guinea
